audit_metadata.scope: Factory Pattern, JSON configuration, API design, extensibility, documentation

ARCH-01.name: JSON Config is Single Source of Truth
findings: Zero hardcoded provider data in code
All 6 providers configured in llm_providers_config.json
Frontend consumes via API, no local duplication
Config file properly loaded and cached in llm_factory.py

ARCH-02.name: llm_factory.get_llm() Extensible for New Providers
findings: Adding new provider: 3 files, ~15 minutes
Each provider follows identical template (7-11 LOC)
No complex branching requiring refactors
Scaling from 7→8 providers adds ~10 LOC

ARCH-03.name: API Response Schema Consistent Across Providers
findings: Streaming format is uniform (data: prefix, SSE)
Frontend has single parser for all providers
Issue: Comparison response lacks per-provider timestamps
Impact: Minor, acceptable for current use case
recommendations: Add 'timestamp': time.time() to each provider result in comparison response

ARCH-04.name: Streaming Response Format Uniform (SSE with data: prefix)
findings: All SSE responses use 'data: ' prefix
Media type correctly set to text/event-stream
No custom protocols or WebSocket usage
Browser can parse with standard EventSource API

ARCH-05.name: Frontend/Backend Contract Documented
findings: 382-line README with comprehensive coverage
All 6 API endpoints documented with examples
Request/Response examples verified against code
Minor gap: timestamp issue should be documented

ARCH-06.name: No Circular Dependencies
findings: Clean dependency hierarchy: Routes → Factory → Providers
Zero circular imports detected
llm_factory.py does not import any routes module
One-way dependencies throughout

ARCH-07.name: Provider Status Enum Standardized
findings: Only returns 'ok' or 'error' (consistent strings)
No ad-hoc status values like 'offline' or 'broken'
Issue: Uses string literals instead of Python Enum class
Impact: Low risk, could be more type-safe
recommendations: Create ProviderStatus Enum with OK and ERROR values

ARCH-08.name: Async/Await Pattern Consistent
findings: All route handlers use async def
All LLM calls use await
Proper asyncio.create_task() for parallel work
Frontend uses await/async for fetch calls
No Promise chains or callback hell

ARCH-09.name: Component State Management (React Hooks) Proper
findings: Proper useState usage throughout
Correct useEffect dependencies
useRef for AbortController (streaming cancellation)
Issue: Could use useReducer for complex state
Impact: Nice-to-have refactoring, not blocking

ARCH-10.name: Documentation Up-to-Date
findings: LLM_PROVIDERS_README.md (382 lines) comprehensive
All endpoints documented with examples
Examples verified to match code
Quick start, troubleshooting, thesis integration covered
Last updated: 2026-04-04

ARCH-B1.name: Separation of Concerns
ARCH-B1.findings: Config → Factory → Routes → UI layers well separated

ARCH-B2.name: Error Handling Strategy
ARCH-B2.findings: Uses HTTPException and ValueError, but generic Exception catches could be more specific

ARCH-B3.name: Retry Logic
ARCH-B3.findings: Fail-fast approach appropriate for testing tool, no automatic retries

ARCH-B4.name: API Versioning
ARCH-B4.findings: Missing /api/v1/ prefix, needed for future API evolution

ARCH-B5.name: Type Hints
ARCH-B5.findings: Backend fully type-hinted, frontend could use JSDoc comments

medium_priority.issue: Generic Exception handling in provider health checks
medium_priority.recommendation: Use specific exception types (ConnectionError, TimeoutError, etc)

low_priority.issue: Per-provider timestamps missing in comparison response
low_priority.recommendation: Add timestamp field to each provider result

low_priority.issue: Status values as strings instead of Enum

low_priority.recommendation: Refactor 9 useState calls into useReducer for clarity

sign_off.next_review: Post-thesis-defense or upon significant scale changes
sign_off.notes: Architecture is sound and extensible. Priority 1 fixes should be implemented before running N>=30 thesis trials to ensure data quality. No critical blockers identified.
